title: dudleya cymosa subsp. minor
intro: Dudleya cymosa subsp. minor, also known as the San Diego liveforever, is a succulent plant species endemic to the coastal areas of southern California. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Dudleya cymosa subsp. minor include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
